facts.text: Founded by Spanish colonists in 1615 as Don Fernando de Taos, the town saw intermittent occupation until the late 1700s when the Pueblo Revolt reshaped its history.
Taos Pueblo remains the only living Native American community that earned both UNESCO World Heritage Site status and National Historic Landmark designation.
The first mission church, Mission de San Gerónimo, was founded around 1610‑12 or 1617, marking the start of Spanish religious architecture in the area.
At Taos Plaza, the American flag flies continuously, day and night, a tradition that reflects the town’s enduring patriotism.
Taos hosts more than twenty sites listed on the National Register of Historic Places, showcasing its rich architectural heritage.
Since 1954, the Helene Wurlitzer Foundation has hosted artist residencies at Casa Encantado, nurturing painters, writers, and filmmakers in Taos.
